notes: The response surfaces genuine risks around fairness methodology adequacy and regulatory position, but fails to name key personas (MRM team, FMA, external validator, head of retail credit) with role-specific costs, does not flag the critical 2023 MRM policy independent validation requirement as a named assumption with risk-if-wrong, conflates SR 11-7 (US) with the FMA/CCCFA NZ regulatory context, provides only one explicit out-of-scope item (retraining/offline evaluation) without reasons for deferral, and lacks anchored success indicators tied to FMA prescribed fairness methodology absolute thresholds.
